contract_claim: The class-level Javadoc says 'the program just needs meaningful code with local variables for the debugger to inspect', which is accurate, but the class name 'InfiniteWait' implies an infinite loop or indefinite blocking. The program actually sleeps for 2 seconds and exits normally. An AI agent reading the name alone will expect an infinite loop and may look for one that doesn't exist.
observed_behavior: The class-level Javadoc says 'the program just needs meaningful code with local variables for the debugger to inspect', which is accurate, but the class name 'InfiniteWait' implies an infinite loop or indefinite blocking. The program actually sleeps for 2 seconds and exits normally. An AI agent reading the name alone will expect an infinite loop and may look for one that doesn't exist.
